title: Kolobopetalum chevalieri
taxonomy: Kolobopetalum chevalieri is a plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae. This classification places it within the larger group of Celastrales, an order of flowering plants. The plant is further categorized as a member of the genus Kolobopetalum, which is a group of flowering plants native to Africa. Kolobopetalum chevalieri is specifically native to the tropical regions of central Africa. Its classification as a member of the Celastraceae family indicates its close relationship with other plants in this family.

The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Kolobopetalum chevalieri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
